title: cymbopogon nardus subvar. citratus
intro: Cymbopogon nardus subvar. citratus, commonly known as lemongrass, is a tropical grass plant native to Sri Lanka and Southeast Asia. This plant is known for its citrusy fragrance and is widely used for its medicinal and culinary properties. The medicinal properties of lemongrass include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which have been used to treat digestive issues, reduce pain and inflammation, and soothe colds and flu. Lemongrass can be consumed in various herbal preparations, such as tea, essential oil, and juice, which have been associated with several health benefits, including weight loss, improved immune function, and reduced stress levels.
